intro: Sift is paid subscription; FingerprintJS is freemium. Sift: Digital trust and safety platform using ML to score account, payment, and content abuse across the customer lifecycle. FingerprintJS: FingerprintJS (Fingerprint Pro) provides browser and mobile device identification with 99.5% accuracy using advanced fingerprinting signals. Its API detects bots, account sharing, multi-accounting and payment fraud, and exposes risk signals. Sift fits marketplace — in practice, high-value transactions. FingerprintJS fits engineering teams adding a reliable device identity signal to their own risk stack. On our rubric Sift scores 4/5 for developer experience and 3/5 for transparency, while FingerprintJS scores 5/5 and 4/5. The honest trade-off: Sift's main drawback — Low transaction volume; FingerprintJS's — Device ID alone does not stop determined fraudsters. For FingerprintJS: 99.5% device ID accuracy, widely benchmarked. Sift is explicitly not the right pick for low transaction volume. FingerprintJS is not aimed at teams that want turnkey approve/decline decisions without building any logic. Another Sift advantage: Marketplace with seller risk.
quick_take: Sift is for marketplace; FingerprintJS is for engineering teams adding a reliable device identity signal; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Sift if your work looks like high-value transactions.
verdict_b: Choose FingerprintJS if engineering teams adding a reliable device identity signal to their own risk stack, and if a free tier with an optional paid path matters.

faq.a: Sift uses paid subscription pricing; FingerprintJS uses freemium. Which is cheaper depends on your volume and team size — model both against your projected usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Sift to FingerprintJS?
faq.a: Expect real effort: Sift is medium lock-in (ML scoring API, integration moderate) and FingerprintJS is medium lock-in (OSS core + Pro API, portable). Migrating between them means rebuilding integrations, re-authoring config, and accepting new coupling. Scope a spike before committing.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Sift or FingerprintJS?
faq.a: FingerprintJS scores higher in our rubric (5/5 vs 4/5 for Sift). Transparency is 4/5 for FingerprintJS and 3/5 for Sift. DX scores are rubric-based, not benchmarks, so evaluate against your own toolchain.

faq.q: Is FingerprintJS a good alternative to Sift?
faq.a: They sit in the same category, so yes — FingerprintJS is a plausible alternative for many Sift use cases. It fits best when engineering teams adding a reliable device identity signal to their own risk stack. Skip it if teams that want turnkey approve/decline decisions without building any logic.
